dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you think Arachne's going to be all right in all of this, my lord? The surface was no longer welcoming, or safe, for her. And now, I fear that Erebus is not much better off...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're right. She's twice as resourceful as just about anybody. Four times! Just... look out for her, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's reassuring to know Artemis has been keeping track of things around here lately, my lord. She seems unusually well-supplied...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Oh, I won't tell a soul.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You picked a fine time to make your deliveries, my lord. That's Artemis singing nearby! Although perhaps such music isn't to your taste.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You can sense it, can't you, my lord? The mark of Primordial Chaos. It seems I have another patron from the ranks of the chthonic gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Nocturnal Arms entrusted me with other Aspects of themselves, including this one that I think was yours, my lord. Did you... relinquish the Moonstone Axe, knowing I was to be its next bearer?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I'll bear it to the best of my ability.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Something about your Gold Rewards... in which I've achieved Silver status now? Oh thank you very much, my lord! I'm pleased to be a loyal customer of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We got him finally, Lord Charon! The Titan can be slain... if but to complicate his schemes a little while, till we can put a stop to him for good.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Yes. My father's throne shall be restored.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I shall, my lord. Until then, we each have our tasks to complete.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It must be so frustrating, Lord Charon. Watching the dead of Erebus wallow here with no place to go. You'll send them on their way yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris returned to the Crossroads, my lord. Forgive me for speaking ill of one of your siblings, but... I always fear the worst when she's around. Could she be working against us?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: If only she was more like you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know already, don't you, my lord? About the Three Fates. That Chronos has your sisters in his thrall. Lord Moros is safe with us, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All of this tainted Gold... you'll see that it's disposed of properly, Lord Charon?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I long to see that night.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, Lord Charon. You'll have what Gold I may find, as I require what supplies you may spare.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Supplying goods to mighty Heracles! Sometimes I wonder whom else you provide for, my lord. Not that you'd say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain he's all right, my lord. This is Hermes we're talking about! It's not like anyone could catch him, even if he were in peril.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I heard from Hermes at last, my lord! He didn't get into the details of what he discovered, though perhaps you know more.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I understand. It's not my wish to pry.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hermes wanted me to tell you hey, my lord. A message he'd surely deliver himself if he could. Though he seems well enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've run into Lord Hermes at the summit of Olympus, making preparations against Typhon with the rest. Have you been able to cross paths with him?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Swiftness...? You mean the Metallic Droplet that I have, my lord. A gift from Hermes himself! No better way to reach your finest wares as soon as possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No sign of Hermes yet, my lord...? Forgive my impatience, I know infiltrating our enemy's ranks and discovering the details of his plans to conquer Olympus is sensitive work...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: He'll be back any evening now I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is it, my lord! The hidden aspect of Nergal that I would never have revealed if not for you. Is this some warrior-god from a distant land, or time?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I bear the gift you generously gave to me. A Keepsake to bring fortune on such nights. Things are looking up already!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This Gold Purse that you handed me before... each night I find it brimming with coins... which must mean technically I'm in your debt.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I met one of your brothers, Lord Charon. It was Moros. He delivered to me a certain artifact, but then was gone. Do you know of the Fated List?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's been in my family's care before...? Though, what of Moros? Where could he have gone in all of this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Charon, Moros returned! He's joined us at the Crossroads. Now we've no fewer than three mighty sons of Nyx within our ranks. Granted, one's always fast asleep...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forgive me, I must not have understood. Hypnos is of greater use this way...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're not particularly close with your brothers or sisters, are you? When you chance to visit us, Nemesis all but ignores you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know that you've seen Nemesis about. She's supposed to be at her post, but... I think you probably can understand. Whatever it takes for the family and the realm, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see you have another customer, my lord. First come first served, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your mother Nyx, my lord... I saw her lately in a nightmare or a dream. She was very beautiful... and she was unharmed, I think.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: We're going to find her. I swear it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, my lord, I met Apollo, Grandmother, Uncle Poseidon... I've known Artemis and Hermes for so long, I'm impressed they kept my secret from the others all this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I made it all the way up to the mountain of the gods, my lord! Though I missed you up there. Never thought I'd see a place you couldn't reach by boat.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: You have a very good distributor.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You sense it too, don't you? A strange feeling in the air... the influence of Chaos all around us. I'm being tested even now. Perhaps all of us are...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We've found a rhythm to these meetings, haven't we? Though is there anything else I can do, my lord? Besides providing all the Gold and the occasional chat.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I did encounter her, my lord. And I thank the Fates Selene's light is visible in Erebus, for I know she cannot shine much farther past the edge of this realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, my lord; I spoke the Oath, if that is what you mean. Thus in shadow, I serve; and to shadow I'll return, ere comes the light of Dawn.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You sense the Testament of Night I think, my lord. So that your mother Nyx's influence may never fade, whether in the Underworld or beyond.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hah! We'll have to see how much Chronos still loves his Gold after all this. The good thing about traitors is their loyalties can be bought...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a pity we've so little opportunity to speak, Lord Charon. Though I'm grateful to be seeing you more frequently of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've come to aid me even here, Lord Charon? Though I imagine not even you can travel much farther, where I'm headed...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Shrines of Lord Hermes that I've seen around the surface... I noticed they take offerings of Gold as well. I take it the two of you are back in business, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You were able to reach the House of Hades before me, my lord. Doubtless why that final stretch was clear. You must know I was unable to prevail. Just have to give it another go, won't we...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean this scythe... you recognize it as your brother's then, my lord. Well I may not be Death Incarnate, but shall wield his blade as deftly as I'm able in his honor.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My Lord, is this your ship? It is remarkable, although I fear with all the hostile vessels everywhere that it may come to harm... or even you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When next we meet in Tartarus, could I not give to you what Gold I have remaining after making my purchases? As a sort of tip for your services. Otherwise it all washes away...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: No goods, no Gold. Got it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've sailed far from your usual course, Boatman. Not solely on my account, I trust?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Your generous aid is much appreciated, lord.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I reached the summit of Olympus finally... they're fighting back against Typhon himself! Perhaps you knew already...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We vanquished Typhon, at least for the night, my lord! Hermes ensured that I was well-supplied. Plenty of Gold for you lately!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The reappearance of your Wells is certainly no accident, my lord. All it took was the appropriate incantation! Little by little, we shall restore the old order.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Charon, why didn't you say anything about those Infernal Contracts I've started to see? I wound up in an inadvertent clash with Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm in your debt, Lord Charon. These provisions surely shall make all the difference in my task. What Gold I find is yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you indulge me for a moment, Boatman? If we're to restore the old order around here, I was thinking why not start like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Indeed, Lord Charon. May we soon restore the old customs between our families, and bring about a new aeon of prosperity.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you require only Gold, my lord. Nevertheless, I wanted to give you something more! I've always found your commitment to the realm and to your discipline inspiring.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perseverance is what got us to this point, my lord. May your resolve never waver! Whenever mine does, I take comfort knowing I have such dependable allies.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You really should try it sometime, my lord! Nectar is one of the things just about everybody on Olympus can agree on, and that must mean something, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you never would permit yourself indulgences, my lord, but... you ought to receive more than mere coin in recompense for all your service! Perhaps the Nectar's but another token gesture.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've heard in times past, you'd discreetly transport shipments of Ambrosia all the way into the depths of Tartarus. And knowing you, my lord, you would never sample your own wares, so here. This is a gift, not a delivery.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: May there soon come such a time...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Here, my lord. If you plan to keep the other Ambrosia bottle in reserve, perhaps you could share this one with a professional associate. No need to save it for later.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I've merely strived to better understand you, knowing it was never about words or gifts. You are the Boatman of the Underworld... a mighty son of Nyx... and the most dependable sort of comrade I could ever want.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Charon, are you giving this to me? The pattern on this parchmentwork... the Mark of Night, and a triple-headed lion, and the words... I see you hoard the shattered corpses of your foes. This is a waking-phrase, for Zorephet?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You are entrusting me with this. Well if you think I'm ready then I'll say it at the Silver Pool next opportunity I get. Thank you, my lord.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A special offer of some sort, my lord? I've returned as much Gold as I could, but know full well it isn't easy for you to procure the goods you provide.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...You mean your sisters the Fates... you knew they'd be all right? I never imagined I could even meet them myself. One of them, she... well, she reminded me a little bit of you.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Well it was only a first impression...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We are victorious indeed, my lord, and thanks in no small part to all you've done. I trust that Nyx has already visited with you. But, what of all the Gold that remains?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why am I unsurprised you're still here offering your wares, even with everything that's happened recently, my lord? You won't stop until the Underworld's entirely restored.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: These shipments shall be very valuable to us. We remain as ever in your debt, Charon. May swift Hermes hasten your voyages across the realms, wherever he may be. And until we meet again, shadows conceal you.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Thank you for the supplies, my lord...!
